rationale: The cited paper appears conceptually central rather than incidental: it studies WOM content differences (recommendations vs details) and sender-side drivers, while the citing paper’s core contribution is about endorsement wording in WOM (explicit recommendations vs implicit statements). The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior work that treated positive WOM as homogeneous, and this 2010 JMRK article is a direct prior on heterogeneity in recommendation-style WOM. With an exact DOI match, this is plausible theory/background scaffolding worth second-stage judgment.
